location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

seniority.level: Senior IC / Director-level Chief of Staff
level_signals: 7+ years experience in a combination of operations, product management, strategy, finance, consulting, government or an equivalent field
Reporting to the Chief Policy Officer
Past experience staffing or working directly with the C-suite/SVP level on a regular basis
Base pay range $156,000 - $195,000 consistent with senior IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years experience in a combination of operations, product management, strategy, finance, consulting, government or an equivalent field

The ideal candidate is a proven leader and has hands-on experience in the following areas: strategic planning, process improvement, project management, financial planning, organizational change, and internal communications
This role requires past experience in staffing or working directly with the C-suite/SVP level on a regular basis
Financial prowess and ability to create budgets and analyze financial models
Proven experience designing and leading strategic planning at a growth company
Ability to comfortably dive into any area of the business or product, understand business needs, and facilitate executive decisions based on changing priorities

Proven ability to track, prioritize, and drive multiple concurrent projects to successful completion. This position is expected to utilize data and metrics to communicate needs with the ease of a business unit owner
Excellent communicator in written and verbal form; ability to work well with executives, and able to lead and influence across multiple levels and multiple functions of an organization
Highest level of integrity, trust, and ability to manage confidential information
Extraordinarily organized, attentive to detail, and and committed to proactively ensuring the completion of all deliverables
Entrepreneurial, adaptable, high tolerance for ambiguity and complexity, and effective with limited resources

scope.surface: Policy team operations and strategic planning under the Chief Policy Officer
primary_metrics_inferred: Delivery of CPO priorities and strategic initiatives
core_responsibilities: Lead day-to-day operations and strategic planning; ranging from administrative approvals and task management, prioritizing meeting schedules, preparing and reviewing materials for executives, developing weekly and quarterly reports, overseeing annual planning and budgeting, and continuous monitoring of organizational design
Communicate priorities, activities, and results to the larger organization, while ensuring deliverables are met
Help build a successful and healthy organization: find knowledge and skills gaps and help address them, design optimal workflows, and align internal processes

Develop a thorough understanding of Product, Operations, Legal, Finance, and overall strategic needs of the company. Based on this, drive the priorities of the CPO by leading weekly team meetings, organizing and running team offsites, and identifying opportunities to increase leadership team effectiveness
Serve as a strategic thought partner to the CPO and Public Policy leadership team
Maintain a pulse on team morale and culture by being the eyes and ears of the CPO within the organization and developing informal and formal feedback loops to identify unmet team needs
Identify and lead special projects to ensure that new, unanticipated, or emerging issues do not fall through the cracks
Gather and analyze relevant data to generate insights, identify trends, and support evidence-based decision-making at the executive level
Collaborate with leadership team and colleagues (People, Finance, etc.) to drive employee satisfaction, headcount efficiency, professional development training, facilities planning, etc.

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not quantified

green_flags: Clear reporting structure (directly to Chief Policy Officer)
Explicit hybrid schedule details (Mon/Wed/Thu in office)
Strong benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave and 401(k) match
Flexibility to work remotely up to 4 weeks per year
red_flags: Role is Washington D.C. area hybrid only — eliminates fully remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned anywhere in the JD
No nice-to-haves listed — hard requirement bar may be inflexible
JD contains a typo ('and and') suggesting lighter editorial review

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, reporting structure, and years requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly in JD)
ambiguities: Nice-to-haves section is entirely absent from this JD; all requirements are framed as requirements or descriptors of the 'ideal candidate', making it difficult to distinguish soft from hard. All flagged requirements have been classified as hard.

The JD mentions 'salary ranges are dependent on a variety of factors, including qualifications, experience and geographic location' suggesting the stated range may vary by location, but only one range (Washington D.C. area) is provided.
